'''''UFC 313: Pereira vs. Ankalaev ''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on March 8, 2025, at [[T-Mobile Arena]] in [[Paradise, Nevada]], part of the [[Las Vegas Valley]], United States.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/lists/ufc-announces-2025-schedule-new-events-location-dates|title=UFC announces 2025 schedule including slew of new events|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2024-11-16|access-date=2024-11-16}}</ref>

==Background==
A [[UFC Light Heavyweight Championship]] bout between current champion (also former [[List of UFC champions#Middleweight Championship|UFC Middleweight Champion]] and former [[Glory (kickboxing)#Glory Middleweight Championship|Glory Middleweight]] and [[Glory (kickboxing)#Glory Light Heavyweight Championship|Light Heavyweight Champion]]) [[Alex Pereira]] and former title challenger [[Magomed Ankalaev]] headlined the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/01/ufc-313-alex-pereira-vs-magomedov-ankalaev-title-fight-headlines|title=Alex Pereira vs. Magomedov Ankalaev title fight headlines UFC 313 in March|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2025-01-18|access-date=2025-01-18}}</ref>

A five-round lightweight bout between former interim [[UFC Lightweight Championship|UFC Lightweight Champion]] (also former [[WSOF Lightweight Championship|WSOF Lightweight Champion]]) [[Justin Gaethje]] and [[Dan Hooker]] was expected to serve as the co-headliner.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/01/ufc-313-justin-gaethje-vs-dan-hooker-five-round-mma-las-vegas|title=Justin Gaethje books date with Dan Hooker at UFC 313 in Las Vegas|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2025-01-18|access-date=2025-01-18}}</ref> However, Hooker had to withdraw from the bout due to a hand injury.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2025/2/25/24372472/dan-hooker-out-of-ufc-313-fight-against-justin-gaethje-with-injury|title=Dan Hooker out of UFC 313 fight against Justin Gaethje with injury|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Ryan Harkness|date=2025-02-25|access-date=2025-02-25}}</ref> He was replaced by [[Rafael Fiziev]] in what became a three-round bout.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2025/02/26/rafael-fiziev-steps-up-to-rematch-justin-gaethje-at-ufc-313/|title=Rafael Fiziev steps up to rematch Justin Gaethje at UFC 313|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Jay Anderson|date=2025-02-26|access-date=2025-02-26}}</ref> They previously met at [[UFC 286]] in March 2023 when Gaethje won by majority decision.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/03/ufc-286-london-results-justin-gaethje-majority-decision-rafael-fiziev-bloody-fight-of-night|title=UFC 286 results: Justin Gaethje bloodies Rafael Fiziev, edges out majority decision|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun|date=2025-03-18|access-date=2025-02-26}}</ref>

A heavyweight bout between [[Jhonata Diniz]] and [[Vitor Petrino]] was expected to take place at the event.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2025-01-02|title=March's UFC 313 gets multiple bookings, including flyweight prospect Joshua Van|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/lists/ufc-313-march-8-fight-card-news-bookings-joshua-van|access-date=2025-01-21|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn}}</ref> However, Petrino was forced to withdraw from the fight due to [[lateral epicondylitis]] in both elbows, so the bout was removed from the card.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2025-01-20 |title=UFC 313 loses heavyweight fight due to double elbow injury|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/1/20/24347152/ufc-313-loses-heavyweight-fight-injury-vitor-petrino-jhonata-diniz|access-date=2025-01-21|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz}}</ref>

A heavyweight bout between former interim [[UFC Heavyweight Championship]] challenger [[Curtis Blaydes]] and promotional newcomer [[Rizvan Kuniev]] was scheduled for [[UFC Fight Night: Cejudo vs. Song]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2024/12/13/24320604/curtis-blaydes-return-set-for-ufc-seattle|title=Curtis Blaydes' return set for UFC Seattle|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2024-12-13|access-date=2025-02-12}}</ref> However, the bout was moved to this event for unknown reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/02/ufc-313-card-curtis-blaydes-vs-rizvan-kuniev-shifted-from-seattle|title=Curtis Blaydes vs. Rizvan Kuniev shifted from Seattle to UFC 313 in Las Vegas|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2025-02-12|access-date=2025-02-12}}</ref> In turn, their bout was cancelled a few hours before the event started as Blaydes suffered from an undisclosed illness.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmanews.com/news/ufc/ufc-313-fights-canceled-including-blaydes/|title=Two UFC 313 fights canceled hours before the event, including Curtis Blaydes' return|publisher=mmanews.com|author=Harvey Leonard|date=2025-03-08|access-date=2025-03-08}}</ref>

A bantamweight bout between [[Chris Gutiérrez]] and undefeated prospect Jean Matsumoto was scheduled for this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/1/5/24336575/ufc-313-adds-bantamweight-scrap-to-march-fight-lineup|title=UFC 313 adds bantamweight scrap to March fight lineup|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Jed Meshew|date=2025-01-05|access-date=2025-02-15}}</ref> However, Matsumoto was withdrawn from this event in order to serve as a replacement fighter against [[Rob Font]] at [[UFC Fight Night: Cejudo vs. Song]] two weeks prior.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/02/ufc-fight-night-252-rob-font-vs-jean-matsumoto-booked|title=Rob Font booked to fight replacement opponent at UFC Seattle|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2024-12-14|access-date=2025-02-15}}</ref> He was replaced by [[John Castañeda]] in a featherweight bout.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://athlonsports.com/mma/ufc-313-chris-gutierrez-vs-john-castaneda|title=UFC 313 fighter takes a risk moving up in weight for high stakes bout|publisher=athlonsports.com|author=Prince Horton|date=2025-03-03|access-date=2025-03-03}}</ref> In turn, the pairing was scrapped hours before taking place as Castañeda suffered an undisclosed illness.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/3/8/24380904/ufc-313-one-fight-cancelled-hours-before-event-kickoff|title=UFC 313: One fight cancelled hours before event kickoff|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Mike Heck|date=2025-03-08|access-date=2025-03-08}}</ref>

[[Bruno Gustavo da Silva]] and [[Joshua Van]] were expected to meet in a flyweight bout on the preliminary card.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/1/2/24334490/ufc-313-lands-new-matchups-march-ppv-event|title=UFC 313 lands 3 new matchups for March event|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz|date=2025-01-02|access-date=2025-01-03}}</ref> However on February 17, Silva pulled out due to undisclosed reasons and was replaced by [[Road to UFC Season 2]] flyweight winner Rei Tsuruya.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/2/17/24367343/bruno-silva-out-of-ufc-313-rei-tsuruya-steps-in-against-joshua-van|title=Bruno Silva out of UFC 313, Rei Tsuruya steps in against Joshua Van|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Jed Meshew|date=2025-02-17|access-date=2025-02-17}}</ref> This was the first ever UFC bout to feature two fighters born in the 2000s.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.si.com/fannation/mma/news/exclusive-23-year-old-joshua-van-reacts-fighting-younger-prospect-rei-tsuruya-ufc-313|title=(Exclusive) 23-year-old Joshua Van reacts to fighting younger prospect at UFC 313|publisher=si.com|author=Christopher De Santiago|date=2025-02-28|access-date=2025-03-08}}</ref>

During the event's broadcast, former [[UFC Welterweight Championship|UFC Welterweight Champion]] [[Robbie Lawler]] was announced as the next "modern wing" [[UFC Hall of Fame]] inductee during International Fight Week festivities in Las Vegas this June. In 2023, Lawler was inducted to the "fight wing" of the Hall of Fame for his title defense against [[Rory MacDonald (fighter)|Rory MacDonald]] at [[UFC 189]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.keviniole.com/2025/03/08/robbie-lawler-named-to-ufc-halloffame|title=Former welterweight champion 'Ruthless' Robbie Lawler named to UFC Hall of Fame|publisher=keviniole.com|author=Kevin Iole|date=2025-03-08|access-date=2025-03-08}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/brianmazique/2025/03/09/ufc-313-results-bonus-winners-highlights-and-reactions/|title=UFC 313 results: bonus winners, highlights and reactions|work=forbes.com|author=Brian Mazique|date=2025-03-09|access-date=2025-03-09}}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night: Justin Gaethje vs. Rafael Fiziev'''
*'''Performance of the Night: Ignacio Bahamondes''' and '''Maurício Ruffy'''
